title: Vitis regeliana
intro: Vitis regeliana, also known as the Manchurian grapevine, is a species of grapevine native to the mountainous regions of China. The health benefits of Vitis regeliana include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation-related diseases. The bioactive constituents of Vitis regeliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic potential. The most common herbal preparations made with Vitis regeliana include teas, extracts, and tinctures, which are often used to promote cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders.
taxonomy: Vitis regeliana is classified within the family Vitaceae and is a species of grapevine native to China. It belongs to the genus Vitis, which consists of various species of grapevines. The family Vitaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the grape family. Within the genus Vitis, Vitis regeliana is often grouped with other species such as Vitis amurensis and Vitis vinifera. These classifications are based on botanical characteristics and genetic analysis.
uses: Vitis regeliana, a species of grapevine, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. In traditional medicine, Vitis regeliana is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. parts: Vitis regeliana, also known as the Regel's grapevine or the Chinese grapevine, is a species of grapevine native to China and parts of the Himalayas. The medicinal parts of Vitis regeliana include its leaves, stems, and roots, which are used in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and are used to treat various conditions, such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
